mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns do not significantly outperform negative returns during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, it signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-stat below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline concurrently, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.1 over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and improving business confidence, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the mechanism predictive accuracy drops below 52 percent in a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic strength and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annualized growth exceeds 4.0 percent, it forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally during high inflation periods, with 60-day forward returns averaging above 0.5 percent when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand and healthy consumer spending, which directly translates to higher corporate earnings forecasts and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of aligned expansion periods, invalidating the demand-transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a divergence where payroll growth slows while the unemployment rate rises signals labor market fragility, triggering short-term equity sell-offs over the next week.
failure_condition: The labor divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns during tightening phases, or the out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45 percent over a 5-year evaluation period.
